title: Array Functions: Adding and Removing
description: Use functions like array_push, array_pop, array_shift, array_unshift.

slides.title: Manipulating Array Elements
slides.content:
# Adding and Removing Elements

PHP provides several functions to work with the beginning and end of arrays.

*   `array_push()` – add one or more elements to the end
*   `array_pop()` – remove the last element
*   `array_unshift()` – add elements to the beginning
*   `array_shift()` – remove the first element

These functions modify the original array.

slides.title: Using array_push and array_pop
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$stack = ["orange", "banana"];
array_push($stack, "apple", "raspberry");
print_r($stack); // ["orange", "banana", "apple", "raspberry"]

$fruit = array_pop($stack); // removes and returns "raspberry"
echo $fruit; // raspberry
print_r($stack); // ["orange", "banana", "apple"]
?>
```

slides.title: Using array_unshift and array_shift
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$queue = ["orange", "banana"];
array_unshift($queue, "apple", "raspberry");
print_r($queue); // ["apple", "raspberry", "orange", "banana"]

$fruit = array_shift($queue); // removes and returns "apple"
echo $fruit; // apple
print_r($queue); // ["raspberry", "orange", "banana"]
?>
```

quizData.question: What does `array_pop()` do?

options.text: Adds an element to the end
Removes the last element and returns it
Removes the first element and returns it

quizData.explanation: `array_pop()` removes and returns the last element of an array.
